policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI mandates all SCBs to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP by March 2027, with physical risk stress testing required annually. Banks must report portfolio-level climate exposure metrics starting Q1 FY27.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Include Scope 3 Emissions for Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI's updated BRSR Core framework mandates reasonable assurance on Scope 3 emissions for top 500 companies by FY27, with value chain mapping requirements. Supply chain climate risk disclosure becomes mandatory for high-emission sectors.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Draft Guidelines for Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI proposes regulatory sandbox for parametric climate products with minimum trigger transparency standards and basis risk disclosure requirements. Insurers can now use satellite and IoT data as primary payout triggers with regulator pre-approval.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: BCBS Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Framework for Global Banks
policy.description: Basel Committee mandates standardized climate risk disclosures for internationally active banks from January 2028, including transition risk metrics and physical risk concentration by geography. Indian banks with foreign operations will need to comply.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: Updated NGFS scenarios now include district-level physical hazard projections for emerging markets including India, with GDP-at-risk estimates through 2100. New acute event frequency models address compound climate hazards.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: EU Confirms CSRD Application to Non-EU Companies with Material India Operations
policy.description: European Commission confirms third-country CSRD requirements apply from FY2028 for non-EU companies with €150M+ EU revenue, affecting Indian IT and pharma majors. Subsidiaries of EU parents must report under full ESRS standards.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for India Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter Intelligence signs multi-year agreement with HDFC Bank to provide ClimateScore Financial for corporate lending portfolio assessment. The partnership marks Jupiter's first major Indian banking client for physical risk modelling.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A to Expand Climate Risk Module for Indian NBFCs
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closes Series A led by Accel India with participation from existing investor Sequoia Surge. Funds will accelerate NBFC-focused climate risk product development and RBI compliance tooling.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India-Specific Physical Risk Scores Covering 600 Districts
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions releases granular physical risk dataset covering flood, cyclone, and heat stress at India district level, integrated with corporate location data. Scores are mapped to PD adjustment factors for credit risk models.

competitors.title: XDI Launches Asset-Level Climate Risk API for Emerging Market Lenders
competitors.description: XDI releases GAIA API targeting banks in India, Southeast Asia, and Latin America with asset-level physical risk scores. API integrates directly with loan origination systems and provides RBI-aligned risk metrics.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Selected by SBI for Natural Capital Valuation of Lending Portfolio
competitors.description: GIST Advisory wins SBI mandate to assess natural capital dependencies across agriculture and infrastructure loan book. Project will pilot TNFD-aligned disclosure for India's largest public sector bank.

competitors.title: Watershed Raises $200M Series D, Announces Emerging Markets Expansion
competitors.description: Watershed closes Series D at $3.5B valuation with plans to open India operations in H2 2026. Company will target large Indian enterprises and their global supply chains for carbon accounting.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Stress Testing Methodologies and Capital Implications
research.description: RBI working paper finds that 18-23% of Indian bank loan portfolios have high physical risk exposure, with agriculture and coastal infrastructure most vulnerable. Paper recommends PD adjustments of 15-40 basis points for high-risk exposures.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Lessons from South Asia Pilots
research.description: World Bank analysis of 12 parametric pilots in India and Bangladesh shows 40% faster claims settlement versus indemnity products. Report identifies basis risk mitigation through hybrid index designs as critical success factor.

research.title: Bridging the ESG Data Gap: Emerging Market Disclosure Challenges and Solutions
research.description: IFC study finds 65% of emerging market companies lack decision-useful climate data, with India showing highest improvement trajectory among BRICS. Report recommends proxy methodologies and satellite-derived datasets for data-sparse contexts.

research.title: sigma 1/2026: Climate Change and Insurance — Global Risks, Regional Impacts
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's annual insured climate losses could reach $8-12B by 2030, up from $3B in 2024. Report highlights protection gap of 85% for flood and 70% for agricultural drought risk.

research.title: Climate-Related Financial Risks: Measurement Methodologies for Emerging Markets
research.description: BIS paper proposes standardized framework for climate-adjusted PD/LGD models suitable for emerging market banks with limited historical data. Framework emphasizes forward-looking hazard models over backward-looking loss data.

research.title: Financing India's Green Transition: Investment Needs and Opportunities to 2030
research.description: CPI analysis identifies $2.5T green investment opportunity in India through 2030, with renewable energy and sustainable agriculture comprising 60% of total. Report notes current tracking of green finance flows covers only 35% of actual activity.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines and global competitors entering India create a 12-month window for StepChange to establish market leadership before commoditization.
body: This month's intelligence paints a clear picture: Indian financial regulators are moving from guidance to mandates, global climate risk players are aggressively entering India, and research increasingly validates the commercial case for climate-adjusted credit risk. The convergence creates both urgency and opportunity for StepChange.

RBI's final climate risk guidelines (policy-1) are the most significant development. The March 2027 ICAAP integration deadline gives banks exactly 12 months to operationalize physical risk stress testing — this is our core value proposition. The RBI working paper (research-1) recommending 15-40 bps PD adjustments for high-risk exposures provides crucial technical validation for our modelling approach. StepChange should immediately develop RBI-aligned stress testing templates and seek pilot engagements with mid-tier banks who lack internal capacity.

The competitor landscape is intensifying rapidly. Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and Moody's India district-level product (comp-3) confirm that global players see India as the next major market. However, Sprih's NBFC focus (comp-2) suggests the Indian SME lender segment remains underserved. StepChange's competitive moat must be India-specific hazard granularity and RBI compliance integration — capabilities global players will take time to develop. The Watershed expansion (comp-6) also signals enterprise ESG competition arriving by year-end.

The parametric insurance opportunity is accelerating faster than expected. IRDAI's sandbox guidelines (policy-3) and the World Bank's positive pilot findings (research-2) create a regulatory and evidence base for product launches. The 40% faster claims settlement stat is powerful sales ammunition. StepChange should fast-track conversations with IRDAI-approved insurers and develop white-label parametric structuring tools that leverage our hazard data.

Watch for: RBI's expected circular on climate risk data standards (likely Q2 2026) which will determine acceptable data sources for regulatory stress tests. Action: Convene a workshop with 3-4 target bank risk heads within 60 days to validate our PD adjustment methodology against the RBI working paper findings — this positions us as a technical partner rather than a vendor.
